issue_title: elasticsearch container start output warn log
issue_body:
**Elasticsearch version** (`bin/elasticsearch --version`): 7.9.2

**Steps to reproduce**: start elasticsearch with docker 

**Description of the problem including expected versus actual behavior**:
I run elasticsarch with default config, container output log like below
```
{"type": "server", "timestamp": "2020-11-13T07:48:57,449Z", "level": "WARN", "component": "o.e.g.DanglingIndicesState", "cluster.name": "docker-cluster", "node.name": "e2333260fcf0", "message": "gateway.auto_import_dangling_indices is disabled, dangling indices will not be automatically detected or imported and must be managed manually" }
```
commit_message:
Lower severity of dangling indices log message (#65309)

Closes #65032.

When the `DanglingIndicesState` component starts, it emits a warning
log message if the `gateway.auto_import_dangling_indices` setting
is `false`. However, this is the default value for this setting,
so the log level should at most be `info`.

commit_message:
Fix trigger assertion error in watcher tests (#65186)

When we trigger watcher in tests we may fail if scheduler is paused.
That can happen when watcher is reloading due to changes in its shards. This is transient state and retry should fix it.
This change adds assertBusy around triggering code so we will try again.

Closes #65183 #65176 #65117 #65091 #65089 #65086 #65064 #65063

commit_message:
Fix trigger assertion error in watcher tests (#65186)

When we trigger watcher in tests we may fail if scheduler is paused.
That can happen when watcher is reloading due to changes in its shards. This is transient state and retry should fix it.
This change adds assertBusy around triggering code so we will try again.

Closes #65183 #65176 #65117 #65091 #65089 #65086 #65064 #65063

commit_message:
Fix trigger assertion error in watcher tests (#65186)

When we trigger watcher in tests we may fail if scheduler is paused.
That can happen when watcher is reloading due to changes in its shards. This is transient state and retry should fix it.
This change adds assertBusy around triggering code so we will try again.

Closes #65183 #65176 #65117 #65091 #65089 #65086 #65064 #65063

commit_message:
Relax asserts in watcher tests (#67404)

When watcher gets reloaded (due to shards routings change) during tests we sometimes get additional watcher history entry (either that the same watch is already queued or that it got throttled because it tried to execute right after previous one).
That trips our assertions which expect exact number of entries.
This change relaxes them to allow exactly-one-more situation and leaves all other checks in place.

Closes #65831
Closes #65176
Closes #65064
Closes #65091

issue_title: EQL: Retrieving async searches occasionally return 404s
issue_body:
**Elasticsearch version**:
7.10

**Description of the problem including expected versus actual behavior**:
When previewing EQL queries via the EQL search strategy in kibana, users will occasionally see an error toast caused by a 404 returned by elasticsearch. The pathology is as follows:

1. User initiates an EQL request (EQL rule preview) that becomes async
2. Client code (search interceptor) receives the async search ID and subsequently requests it
3. Elasticsearch returns a 404 for said ID, causing client code to perform a request “cancellation”
4. Request cancellation comes in the form of a DELETE request for said ID, which returns a 200.

I believe the DELETE request succeeding is evidence that the query exists but is temporarily unavailable at the time it is requested.

 ~~This seems related to [this issue](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/63652), and the current theory is that a similar fix is needed for EQL's async implementation (if it in fact differs).~~ Update: nope, that issue is unrelated.


**Steps to reproduce**:

As this all happens in quick succession, it is hard to reproduce manually. Additionally, the error manifests sporadically, so reproduction steps are fuzzy at this point. However, the behavior has been observed by multiple users in multiple environments, and **I've got a deployment in cloud that can demonstrate the issue relatively reliably**. A few other speculative data points that may be helpful:

* I've been unable to reproduce the issue without data
   * seemingly because the query is quick and completes synchronously
* Slower, more expensive queries _seem_ to increase the likelihood of manifesting the behavior. 

**Provide logs (if relevant)**:
Within kibana, the 404 is logged from the client: 

```
server    log   [10:46:29.496] [error][data][elasticsearch] [resource_not_found_exception]: FlN0bEJtS3czUnhHZDdvTHdQOHJIQlEfNnpLdk1MbTNSUU9sQnV6SF83NEExZzo1NTIxOTgwNw==
```
commit_message:
Fix "resource not found" exception on existing EQL async search (#65167)

This change fixes the initialization of the async results service
for the EQL get async action. The boolean that differentiates EQL
from normal _async_search request is set incorrectly, which results
in errors (404) when extending the keep alive of a running EQL search.

Fixes #65108

issue_title: index.routing.allocation.include  not work as document described
issue_body:
**Elasticsearch version** (`bin/elasticsearch --version`):
7.2.0

**Plugins installed**: []

**JVM version** (`java -version`):
java version "1.8.0_91"


**OS version** (`uname -a` if on a Unix-like system):
Linux   2.6.32-642.6.2.el6.x86_64 #1 SMP Wed Oct 26 06:52:09 UTC 2016 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux



**Description of the problem including expected versus actual behavior**:

as the document say that different attributes for ``` index.routing.allocation.include.xxx ``` are all should satisfied
>If you specify multiple filters, all conditions must be satisfied for shards to be relocated. For example, to move the test index to big nodes in rack1, you could specify:

```
PUT test/_settings
{
  "index.routing.allocation.include.size": "big",
  "index.routing.allocation.include.rack": "rack1"
}
```
the document is [here](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/7.2/shard-allocation-filtering.html#index-allocation-filters) 

but in my test i find that the test index will be relocated to nodes that have ever ``` big ```size OR  ```rack1``` attr

if i want to move the test index to big nodes in rack1, i should do like this 

```
PUT test/_settings
{
  "index.routing.allocation.require.size": "big",
  "index.routing.allocation.require.rack": "rack1"
}
```
commit_message:
Fix Allocation `include` Filter Docs (#65202)

Fix documentation to match actual behavior of `include` type filters.

Closes #65113

issue_title: The set processor introduces unwanted escape characters (backslashes) when using templated fields
issue_body:
<!--
GitHub is reserved for bug reports and feature requests; it is not the place
for general questions. If you have a question or an unconfirmed bug , please
visit the [forums](https://discuss.elastic.co/c/elasticsearch).  Please also
check your OS is [supported](https://www.elastic.co/support/matrix#show_os).
If it is not, the issue is likely to be closed.

For security vulnerabilities please only send reports to security@elastic.co.
See https://www.elastic.co/community/security for more information.

Please fill in the following details to help us reproduce the bug:
-->

**Elasticsearch version** (`bin/elasticsearch --version`): 

7.9.3 on Elastic cloud

**Plugins installed**: []

**JVM version** (`java -version`):

**OS version** (`uname -a` if on a Unix-like system):

**Description of the problem including expected versus actual behavior**:

The set processor introduces unwanted escape characters (backslashes) when using templated fields. The behaviour has been observed for double quotes (`"`)

**Steps to reproduce**:

Input:

```
POST _ingest/pipeline/_simulate
{
  "pipeline": {
    "processors": [
      {
        "set": {
          "field": "message2",
          "value": "{{message}}"
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  "docs": [
    {
      "_source" : {
      "message" : """10.10.1.1 - - [17/Nov/2020:04:59:43 +0000] "GET /info HTTP/1.1" 200 6229 "-" "-"  2"""
      }
    }
    ]
}
```

Output:

```
{
  "docs" : [
    {
      "doc" : {
        "_index" : "_index",
        "_type" : "_doc",
        "_id" : "_id",
        "_source" : {
          "message" : """10.10.1.1 - - [17/Nov/2020:04:59:43 +0000] "GET /info HTTP/1.1" 200 6229 "-" "-"  2""",
          "message2" : """10.10.1.1 - - [17/Nov/2020:04:59:43 +0000] \"GET /info HTTP/1.1\" 200 6229 \"-\" \"-\"  2"""
        },
        "_ingest" : {
          "timestamp" : "2020-11-17T05:26:12.174346581Z"
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```
commit_message: Configurable MIME type for mustache template encoding on set processor (#65314)

commit_message:
Fix trigger assertion error in watcher tests (#65186)

When we trigger watcher in tests we may fail if scheduler is paused.
That can happen when watcher is reloading due to changes in its shards. This is transient state and retry should fix it.
This change adds assertBusy around triggering code so we will try again.

Closes #65183 #65176 #65117 #65091 #65089 #65086 #65064 #65063

commit_message:
Better mimick real reduction in test

This changes has `InternalAggregationTestCase` performs partial
reductions to better mimick `InternalAggregations#reduce`. In
particular, this fixes a bug when we'd attempt a partial reduction of
aggregations that were entirely unmapped but use a mapped aggregation as
the "lead" of the reduction. This was possible in the test but isn't
possible in production. The reduction "lead" is unmapped if all agg
results being reduced are unmapped.

Closes #65118

Watcher understands hidden expand wildcard value (#65332)

Watcher has a search template that stores indices options to be used as
part of a search during watch execution, but this was not updated to be
aware of hidden indices and the `hidden` expand_wildcards option. This
change makes use of the `IndicesOptions#toXContent` method in Watcher,
which already handles the new value. Additionally, the XContent parsing
is moved to the IndicesOptions class so that we will be less likely to
miss updating this in the future.

Closes #65148

Fix TimeSeriesLifecycleActionsIT.testForceMergeActionWithCompressionCodec (#67309)

possible that we may not reach that count. Rather, we should check the codec setting to ensure that
it has been correctly changed

Resolves #65171

commit_message:
Relax asserts in watcher tests (#67404)

When watcher gets reloaded (due to shards routings change) during tests we sometimes get additional watcher history entry (either that the same watch is already queued or that it got throttled because it tried to execute right after previous one).
That trips our assertions which expect exact number of entries.
This change relaxes them to allow exactly-one-more situation and leaves all other checks in place.

Closes #65831
Closes #65176
Closes #65064
Closes #65091

issue_title: [ML] ML Initialisation Service should detect non-retryable errors
issue_body:
As an example `MlInitializationService` will create the `.ml-annotations-6` index and `.ml-annotations-write` alias if they does not exist but if an accidental write to the alias has created a concrete index this will error:
```
[ERROR][o.e.x.m.MlInitializationService] [HOST] Error creating ML annotations index or aliases
org.elasticsearch.indices.InvalidAliasNameException: Invalid alias name [.ml-annotations-write], an index exists with the same name as the alias
```

Unfortunately this message along with a stack trace will be logged on cluster change events creating a lot of log spam.

In this case the error is non-recoverable, the service should notice this and not retry either until `.ml-annotations-write` does not exist anymore or a new master node is elected at which point the new master has not noticed the error previously and will try.
commit_message:
[ML] reduce the logging spam for the ml initializer for annotation index creation (#80149)

It is possible that the annotation index is in an unsupported state. Consequently, the same exception is thrown whenever the MlInitializationService attempts to create the index.

Logging all those failures at the error level doesn't make sense. This commit only logs the first seen (of duplicate errors) as error and the rest of debug.

Whenever the error changes, it is logged.

closes #65217

issue_title: _mtermvectors is perhaps overly strict w.r.t. aliases and concrete indices
commit_message:
Better handling of item errors in _mtermvectors API (#65324)

Currently an error in a `_mtermvectors`, for example because querying through an
alias that has several indices assigned to it, fails the whole request. Instead
we should only fail the problematic item in the multi item request, like we e.g.
do in same situations in _mget.

issue_body: In #64841 started validating the codebases existed in our security policy. This broke running tests in Eclipse because we include a special policy for IntelliJ if we're not running in gradle. Eclipse doesn't run in gradle and doesn't have the IntelliJ policy.
commit_message:
Use different security policies for eclipse vs intellij (#65234)

Intellij has a special junit runner jar that needs additional security
manager permissions. However, this jar is not present in eclipse. This
commit adds a new (empty) policy for eclipse, and then chooses which to
load based on the presence of the special intellij junit jar. This is a
bit of a hack, but there are no special system properties for intellij
or eclipse that I could find (there are substrings that could be
searched for in existing properties, but looking for a specific key is
much easier to do that searching a substring).

relates #65231

issue_title: Synced flush deprecation message issue in v7.9.2
issue_body:
<!--
GitHub is reserved for bug reports and feature requests; it is not the place

If it is not, the issue is likely to be closed.

For security vulnerabilities please only send reports to security@elastic.co.
See https://www.elastic.co/community/security for more information.

Please fill in the following details to help us reproduce the bug:
-->

**Elasticsearch version** (`bin/elasticsearch --version`):
7.9.2

**Plugins installed**: []
n/a
**JVM version** (`java -version`):
n/a
**OS version** (`uname -a` if on a Unix-like system):
n/a
**Description of the problem including expected versus actual behavior**:
On successful synced flush the deprecation warning improperly suggests `_/flush` instead of `/_flush`
The deprecation warning does not show up on failures, yet this would be useful since it would encourage the user to not to keep retrying as they are used to doing and use the preferred method.

**Steps to reproduce**:
 1. Run POST _flush/synced
 2. If shards failed, HTTP 409 - Conflict returned.  No deprecation warning.
 3. If success, HTTP 200, then the following warning:
`#! Deprecation: Synced flush is deprecated and will be removed in 8.0. Use flush at _/flush or /{index}/_flush instead.
`
**Provide logs (if relevant)**:
commit_message:
Fix deprecation message for synced flush (#66182)

This commit fixes a typo for the deprecation message that synced flush
emits and also updates a test to always confirm that the deprecation
message is returned.

This is the same commit from merging #66130, which was reverted due to
causing failures on master because of the message change.

Relates #65235

issue_title: [REST API] Requests with unconsumed bodies should be rejected.
issue_body:
# Summary
This is a regression of https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/37504. 

# Environment
Kibana: 7.x (7.11)
Elasticsearch: 7.x (7.11)

# How To Test
Run the following command in console.
```
PUT test/_doc/1
{}
DELETE /test
{
  "query" : {
    "term" :  {
      "field" : "value"
    }
  }
}
```
The above is no longer rejected.  

Another example (which found this regression): 
```
POST /latency/_forcemerge
{
  "only_expunge_deletes": true,
  "max_num_segments": 1
}
```
Which since https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/37504 should not be allowed. (related: https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/30792)

# Screenie
http://g.recordit.co/bUGDVNyJue.gif
commit_message:
RestController should not consume request content (#66043)

The change #37504 modifies the BaseRestHandler to make it reject all requests
that have an unconsumed body. The notion of consumed or unconsumed body
 is carried by the RestRequest object and its contentConsumed attribute, which
 is set to true when the content() or content(true) methods are used.

In our REST layer, we usually expect the RestHandlers to consume the request
content when needed, but it appears that the RestController always consumes
 the content upfront.

This commit changes the content() method used by the RestController so that it
does not mark the content as consumed.

Backport of #44902
Closes #65242

Co-authored-by: Tanguy Leroux <tlrx.dev@gmail.com>

issue_title: Confusing steps in internode TLS setup docs
issue_body:
In https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/configuring-tls.html we have:

> 4. Copy the node certificate to the appropriate locations.
>
>   a.  Create a folder in the configuration directory on each Elasticsearch node to contain your security certificates. For example, create a certs folder in the /home/es/config/certs directory.
> > The Elasticsearch configuration directory varies depending on your Elasticsearch installation.
>
>  b.  Copy the node certificates into the certs directory that you created in the previous step.
>  c.  Copy the .p12 keystore file into the Elasticsearch configuration directory. Elasticsearch will fail to start if the keystore file is located anywhere except this directory.
>  d.  For each additional Elastic product that you want to configure, copy the certificates to the relevant configuration directory.

It looks like this exact wording was introduced in https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/63334 when we copied some wording from one section to another, but it doesn't quite land right here.

Steps (b) and (c) are saying more or less the same thing with different words. That can confuse the reader because they assume (c) is asking them to do something additional, but they can't work out what it is.  
I suspect (b) is intended to cover node certificates (from step 2) and (c) is intended to cover the HTTP certificate store (from step 3), but that's not clear.  Perhaps the solution is to explicitly refer to those steps? Something like:

> b. On each node, copy the certificates that you created in step 2, into the `certs` directory that you created in the previous step. This will typically be a single ".p12" (PKCS#12) file
>
> c. If you chose to generate HTTP certificates (step 3), then copy the `http.p12` file into the `certs` directory as well.


_I don't like using "typically" in (b), but going into more explanation is hard, and I'm trying to avoid making big changes, since I know @lockewritesdocs is working on an full overhaul of these instructions.

should ignore that when checking the list of files against known segment stats.

commit_message:
Fix SearchableSnapshotsIntegTests.testCreateAndRestoreSearchableSnapshot (#65343)

The recovery stats assertions in this test ran without any waiting for
the recoveries to actually finish. The fact that they ran after the concurrent
searches checks generally meant that they would pass (because of searches warming caches
+ general relative slowness of searches) but there is no hard guarantees this will work
reliably as the pre-fetch threads which will update the recovery state might still be slow
to do so randomly, causing the assertions to trip.

closes #65302

issue_title: "exists" query on geo_point property with doc_values=false incorrectly matches documents with a null value
commit_message:
Fix handling of null values in geo_point (#65307)

A bug was introduced in 7.10 that causes explicit `null` values to be indexed in the _field_names
field. This change fixes this bug for newly ingested data but `null` values ingested with 7.10 will
continue to match `exists` query so a reindex is required.

Fixes #65306

issue_title: Metricbeat errors due to missing `manage` permission for `remote_monitoring_user` user
issue_body:
This bug is similar to #63203.

Metricbeat configured with user `remote_monitoring_user` fails on fresh Elasticsearch 7.10 install with security enabled with error:

```
Failed to connect to backoff(elasticsearch(http://localhost:9200)): Connection marked as failed because the onConnect callback failed: failed to create alias: {"error":{"root_cause":[{"type":"security_exception","reason":"action [indices:admin/aliases] is unauthorized for user [remote_monitoring_user]"}],"type":"security_exception","reason":"action [indices:admin/aliases] is unauthorized for user [remote_monitoring_user]"},"status":403}: 403 Forbidden: {"error":{"root_cause":[{"type":"security_exception","reason":"action [indices:admin/aliases] is unauthorized for user [remote_monitoring_user]"}],"type":"security_exception","reason":"action [indices:admin/aliases] is unauthorized for user [remote_monitoring_user]"},"status":403}
```

Here is the request:

```HTTP
PUT /%3Cmetricbeat-7.10.0-%7Bnow%2Fd%7D-000001%3E HTTP/1.1
Host: localhost:9200
User-Agent: Go-http-client/1.1
Content-Length: 58
Accept: application/json
Authorization: Basic xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8
Accept-Encoding: gzip

{"aliases":{"metricbeat-7.10.0":{"is_write_index":true}}}
```

And response:

```HTTP
HTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden
content-type: application/json; charset=UTF-8
content-encoding: gzip
content-length: 156

{"error":{"root_cause":[{"type":"security_exception","reason":"action [indices:admin/aliases] is unauthorized for user [remote_monitoring_user]"}],"type":"security_exception","reason":"action [indices:admin/aliases] is unauthorized for user [remote_monitoring_user]"},"status":403}
```

Creating another user with roles `remote_monitoring_agent` and `remote_monitoring_collector` and temporary role with `manage` permission for index `metricbeat-*` solves the problem. I think that `remote_monitoring_agent` should have `manage` permission added.
commit_message:
Append priv for _aliases over metricbeat-* for monitoring agent (#69212)

This adds the privilege for the `_aliases` action for the `metricbeat-*` index
namespace to the `remote_monitoring_agent` built-in role (used by metricbeat
agents). This is required (at least) when creating ingest indices that have aliases.

Notably, this change permits metricbeat agents to delete indices via the
`remove_index` action, which is undesirable but we have no way around it,
at the moment.

Correctly serialize search-as-you-type analyzer (#65359)

The search-as-you-type mapper was using an incorrect default analyzer when
creating its merge builder, which meant that a configured analyzer would not
be included in serialization.  This in turn resulted in the master node
getting an incorrect configuration, and search-as-you-type fields always
using a standard analyzer.

This has already been fixed via subsequent refactorings in 7.11 and master,
so this fix is for 7.10 only.

Resolves #65319

issue_title: SQL query not properly applying some operators
commit_message:
{S,E}QL: Fix optimization of `NotEquals` in conjunctions (#65331)

* Fix the `CombineBinaryComparisons` optimizer rule, so that semantic
equality taken into account during the optimization of `NotEquals`

Examples that previously removed the `NotEquals` expressions (leading
to incorrect results):

```
double >= 10 AND integer != 9            -->  double >= 10

keyword != '2021'
  AND datetime >= '2020-01-01T00:00:00'  -->  keyword != '2021'
```

With the fix, expressions like the above will not be touched.
`NotEquals` will only be eliminated from the `AND` expression if the
left side of the `NotEquals` `semanticEquals()` to the left side
of the other expressions within the conjunction (comparisons against
the same field/expression).

* Unit tests and integration tests

Close #65322

issue_title: Dotted named field values can merge with non-dotted fields of the same name
commit_message:
Add test for previously broken behaviour on dotted array field (#85081)

Previously, when using dynamic: false, an array field with a dot in its name, whose suffix matched
a mapped field’s name, had its values merged with the mapped field unexpectedly. This has been fixed by #79922

This commit adds a test for that scenario and verifies that the bug is fixed.

Closes #65333

commit_message:
[ML] fixes minor snapshot upgrader task failure bug and rest client test failure (#65607)

This commit fixes two issues:

- On task failure and when the task has completed, there is no reason to utilize the single queued executor. 
- The high level rest client tests should "await_completion" to make sure the task has completed before allowing other tests to continue.

closes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/65364

commit_message:
Fix AbstractClient#execute Listener Leak (#65415)

This was observed in #65405 due to trying to locally execute a
task whose parent was already cancelled but is a general issue.

We should not throw from APIs that consume a listener as this may
like in this case leak the listener in that case.
Rather than fixing the specific case of #65405 this fixes the
abstract client overall to avoid other such leaks.

Closes #65405

issue_title: Specifying the default_search analyzer no longer works for Elasticsearch 7.10.0
commit_message:
Restore use of default search and search_quote analyzers (#65491)

In the refactoring of TextFieldMapper, we lost the ability to define
a default search or search_quote analyzer in index settings. This
commit restores that ability, and adds some more comprehensive
testing.

Fixes #65434

commit_message:
Fixes GeoLineAggregator bugs (#65521)

This commit unmutes and fixes tests around some GeoLineAggregator edge
cases.

- MergedGeoLines had a silly bug where it was accepting InternalGeoLines
  that were empty
- "complete" is measured by the heap-mode of the BucketedSort, which is a problem
  since if the length of the data equals the max-size, then it is difficult to know
  whether any values were discarded.
- GeoLineBucketSort had an array-resizing bug s/>/>=

Fixes #65473.

issue_body:
In case the filtering (`WHERE`) clause of an SQL query contains at least two relational operators in a conjunction (`AND`) of which one is a comparison (`<=`, `<`, `>=` or `>`) and another is an inequality (`!=` or `<>`), the inequality can get ignored. 

```
SELECT MIN(emp_no) FROM test_emp WHERE emp_no != 10001 AND salary > 50000 
```
will wrongly return 10001.

This will happen despite the fact that the comparisons are done on different terms and only when the _values_ of the comparisons are literals or foldable expressions such that the inequality's value lies outside the range of the comparison; in this case an incorrect optimisation is applied.

A workaround in this case is to replace the inequality with an `NOT IN` operator:

```
SELECT MIN(emp_no) FROM test_emp WHERE emp_no NOT IN (10001) AND salary > 50000
```

issue_title: Fields API won't extract field that begins with the name of an object
commit_message:
Correctly handle mixed object paths in XContentMapValues (#65539)

When we have an object that looks like this:

```
{
  "foo" : {
    "cat": "meow"
  },
  "foo.bar" : "baz"
```

where the inner objects of foo are defined both as json objects and via
dot notation, then XContentMapValues.extractValue() will only descend
through the json object and will not collect values from the dot notated paths.
In these cases, the foo.bar values will not be returned in the fields
section of a search response.

This commit adds the ability to check both paths, ensuring that all relevant
values get returned as part of fields.

Fixes #65499

issue_title: Get snapshot repository API returns 404 when no repository
issue_body:
**Elasticsearch version**:
Current es snapshot 
https://storage.googleapis.com/kibana-ci-es-snapshots-daily/8.0.0/archives/20201124-194417_1ce323e1368/elasticsearch-8.0.0-SNAPSHOT-darwin-x86_64.tar.gz 

**OS version**:
macos Catalina 10.15.7

**Description of the problem including expected versus actual behavior**:
Get snapshot repository returns a 404 error for this request `GET /_snapshot/*`.
```
{
  "error" : {
    "root_cause" : [
      {
        "type" : "repository_missing_exception",
        "reason" : "[*] missing"
      }
    ],
    "type" : "repository_missing_exception",
    "reason" : "[*] missing"
  },
  "status" : 404
}
```

Both `GET /_snapshot` and `GET /_snapshot/_all` work and return an empty object. When a repository was created and then deleted, the endpoint works as expected. 

**Steps to reproduce**:

 1. Start Kibana and ES with `yarn start` and `yarn es snapshot -E path.repo=./my_repo`
 2. Navigate to Dev Tools and send `GET /_snapshot/*` (returns a 404 error)
 3. Then send 2 following requests to created and delete a repository 
```
PUT /_snapshot/my_repository
{
  "type": "fs",
  "settings": {
    "location": "./my_repo"
  }
}

DELETE /_snapshot/my_repository 
```
4. Send again `GET /_snapshot/*` (now the request works)
commit_message:
Fix Inconsistent API Behavior when Cluster never had a Snapshot Repository Configured (#65535)

The behavior of APIs was different depending on whether or not a cluster ever had any
repository configured due to the way we handled the `Custom` `null` values.
Added a default value overload for `#custom` the same way we have it for
custom non-metadata CS values and simplified code accordingly.

Closes #65511

issue_title: [DOCS] Explanation for columns returned in _cat/allocation API
issue_body:
<!--
GitHub is reserved for bug reports and feature requests; it is not the place
for general questions. If you have a question or an unconfirmed bug , please
visit the [forums](https://discuss.elastic.co/c/elasticsearch).  Please also
check your OS is [supported](https://www.elastic.co/support/matrix#show_os).
If it is not, the issue is likely to be closed.

For security vulnerabilities please only send reports to security@elastic.co.
See https://www.elastic.co/community/security for more information.

Please fill in the following details to help us reproduce the bug:
-->

**Elasticsearch version** (`bin/elasticsearch --version`): N/A

**Plugins installed**: N/A

**JVM version** (`java -version`): N/A

**OS version** (`uname -a` if on a Unix-like system): N/A

**Description of the problem including expected versus actual behavior**: 

[The `GET _cat/allocation?v` API](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/cat-allocation.html) currently shows the below columns:
```
shards disk.indices disk.used disk.avail disk.total disk.percent host      ip        node
     1         260b    47.3gb     43.4gb    100.7gb           46 127.0.0.1 127.0.0.1 CSUXak2
```

Though there is no concrete description of _exactly_ what each of these headings is measuring. Things like `shards`/`disk.total` are self explanatory, there appears to be some subtle difference between the values reported in `disk.indices` and `disk.used`. 

If we run `GET _cat/allocation?help` for some details we get:
```
shards       | s              | number of shards on node      
disk.indices | di,diskIndices | disk used by ES indices       
disk.used    | du,diskUsed    | disk used (total, not just ES)
disk.avail   | da,diskAvail   | disk available                
disk.total   | dt,diskTotal   | total capacity of all volumes 
disk.percent | dp,diskPercent | percent disk used             
host         | h              | host of node                  
ip           |                | ip of node                    
node         | n              | name of node
```

However, it's still 100% clear if `disk.indices` takes into consideration indices that have shards that are `UNASSIGNED`, for example nodes that have some data on disk for shards, but cannot allocate them due to tripping something like `cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark.*`. 

Checking [the source code](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/master/server/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/rest/action/cat/RestAllocationAction.java#L142), it appears as though this is indeed what's happening.

Can we update the docs for `GET _cat/allocation?v` to: 
1. Call out the above scenario of `disk.indices` vs `disk.used` in cases where shard data might exist on disk, but cannot be allocated due to circuit breakers, disk watermarks etc.
2. Document the columns like we do via `GET _cat/allocation?help`, in similar format to what we've done for [`GET _cat/nodes`](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/cat-nodes.html)?

If we can get a concrete answer to the scenario mentioned in Item 1, then I'd be happy to raise a PR myself.

Thanks folks ❤️
commit_message: [DOCS] Document cat allocation API response properties (#65635)

issue_title: FVH Highlighter does not work with the inner hits properly
commit_message:
Fix bug where fvh fragments could be loaded from wrong doc (#65641)

This PR fixes a regression where fvh fragments could be loaded from the wrong
document _source.

Some `FragmentsBuilder` implementations contain a `SourceLookup` to load from
_source. The lookup should be positioned to load from the current hit document.
However, since `FragmentsBuilder` are cached and shared across hits, the lookup
is never updated to load from the new documents. This means we accidentally
load _source from a different document.

The regression was introduced in #60179, which started storing `SourceLookup`
on `FragmentsBuilder`.

Fixes #65533.

commit_message:
Stricter Check for Snapshot Restore Version Compatibility (#65580)

We can add a pre-flight check for version compatibility that uses the
version in `SnapshotInfo` so that we don't need to load the `INdexMetadata`
for this which may not be readable 2 major versions back to begin with.

Note: this still technically leaves the option where a snapshot of major N contains
indices of major `N - 1` but sine we don't have any way of reading the index meta version
other than reading the actual `IndexMetadata` there isn't much we can do about this without
investing non-trivial efforts -> I think this is a good enough enhancement to the checks
for now.

Closes #65567

commit_message:
Allow yaml values for dynamic node settings (#85186)

Environment variables are allowed as substitutions within
elasticsearch.yml. Additionally, command line settings are added into
the parsed settings. However, both of these are raw strings applied
after the node yml file has been parsed.

This commit moves environment substitution to occur before parsing
elasticsearch.yml, and override processing to happen with a separate
yaml parser so that both can allow yaml parsing. Note that environment
substitution is not the only type of substitution (there is also setting
substitution, which needs parsed setting keys), so the existing
replacement mechanism is not touched here except to remove environment
handling.

closes #65577

commit_message:
Try to make DeprecationHttpIT more robust (#65665)

Closes #65589 hopefully. There is a test case that checks whether
deprecation logs have been indexed. It uses `assertBusy` so that it
keeps checking for a period, since it may take some time for the data to
become available. However, the test nonetheless occasionally still
fails, with no shards being available to search.

In an attempt to address this, add a `_refresh` call on the deprecation
data stream, and increase the `assertBusy` timeout to 30s.
